subject: CI/CD vFirewall Close Loop
commit_message:
CI/CD vFirewall Close Loop

Change onap network cidr to /16 due to robot hardcoded config.
Adding onap network id to configuration at installation part.
Creating ansible playbook to perform vFWCL for pipeline.

Issue-ID: OOM-1996
Signed-off-by: Denis Kasanic <d.kasanic@partner.samsung.com>
Change-Id: I925cb520ad936e215c398f2e430d5d8854344dac

entries.message:
Patch Set 1:

This change is ready for review.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/offline-installer-master-review/680/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/offline-installer-master-review/680

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Testing onap demos is content that does not directly belong to offline-installer and even less into cicdansible tool, where this commit is placing it.

Would be better to host these test in onap's demo repository. Maybe implement it as Ansible-galaxy collection https://galaxy.ansible.com/docs/contributing/creating_collections.html. Anyway separated from both cicdansible and offline-installer ansible code base.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Testing onap demos is content that does not directly belong to offline-installer and even less into cicdansible tool, where this commit is placing it.
> 
> Would be better to host these test in onap's demo repository. Maybe implement it as Ansible-galaxy collection https://galaxy.ansible.com/docs/contributing/creating_collections.html. Anyway separated from both cicdansible and offline-installer ansible code base.

If I understand my task correctly, I am not doing vFWCL itself, but only jenkins pipeline for it and only in our jenkins. Then Onap's demo repo is not good place. However, ci-management repo neither as this pipeline will not be present in onap upstream jenkins.

If you persist on putting it ci-management repo, shouldn't be then whole cicdansible tool also placed in onap's ci-management repository?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > Testing onap demos is content that does not directly belong to offline-installer and even less into cicdansible tool, where this commit is placing it.
> > 
> > Would be better to host these test in onap's demo repository. Maybe implement it as Ansible-galaxy collection https://galaxy.ansible.com/docs/contributing/creating_collections.html. Anyway separated from both cicdansible and offline-installer ansible code base.
> 
> If I understand my task correctly, I am not doing vFWCL itself, but only jenkins pipeline for it and only in our jenkins. Then Onap's demo repo is not good place. However, ci-management repo neither as this pipeline will not be present in onap upstream jenkins.
> 
> If you persist on putting it ci-management repo, shouldn't be then whole cicdansible tool also placed in onap's ci-management repository?

Yes it's not vFW itself but support to run automatically vFW demo in the context of offline onap deployment (or prob/hopefully also online onap).

Our pipeline should be present in onap upstream Jenkins (that has been all the time our target).

cicdansible tool is already upstreamed and better place for it could be in ci-management. Expecially if it's somehow impossible otherwise to get pipeline to onap Jenkins. But I don't in general see reason why cicdansible could not be also in current repo.

But the point of my comment, that where this running onap demo code should be, is still same. It does not belong to cicdansible, offline-installer code base directly, but is content that can be executed by these tools. And content very tightly is related to the demo itself.


One more tactical comment. The code seems to be very simple and short so there's prob no need to create roles out of this as there's no usage of those roles elsewhere. So whole demo run maybe could be a single ansible playbook having all code.

> But the point of my comment, that where this running onap demo code should be, is still same. It does not belong to cicdansible, offline-installer code base directly, but is content that can be executed by these tools. And content very tightly is related to the demo itself.
> 
> 
> One more tactical comment. The code seems to be very simple and short so there's prob no need to create roles out of this as there's no usage of those roles elsewhere. So whole demo run maybe could be a single ansible playbook having all code.

ok, I understand your point. For the sake of simplicity, is it possible to put this ansible playbook somewhere in offline-installer? I can try to get pipeline/code merged also to ci-management, but changes are required regarding onap config. There are tasks specific to our lab, e.g cloud-init patch for VNFs.

> > cicdansible tool is already upstreamed and better place for it could be in ci-management. Expecially if it's somehow impossible otherwise to get pipeline to onap Jenkins. But I don't in general see reason why cicdansible could not be also in current repo.
> > 
> > But the point of my comment, that where this running onap demo code should be, is still same. It does not belong to cicdansible, offline-installer code base directly, but is content that can be executed by these tools. And content very tightly is related to the demo itself.
> > 
> > 
> > One more tactical comment. The code seems to be very simple and short so there's prob no need to create roles out of this as there's no usage of those roles elsewhere. So whole demo run maybe could be a single ansible playbook having all code.

> ok, I understand your point. For the sake of simplicity, is it possible to put this ansible playbook somewhere in offline-installer? I can try to get pipeline/code merged also to ci-management, but changes are required regarding onap config. There are tasks specific to our lab, e.g cloud-init patch for VNFs.

There's no good place for it in offline-installer. Offline-installer has functionality to run custom actions (user's own ansible roles) automatically, but it's designed to perform custome actions/modifications to the system not to run tests against it.

I'm not familiar with cicdansible but I think it could implement similar plugin functionality to execute user's actions even tests against installed application. Quickly looking at code seems now that application is a bit hardcoded to be onap.

Tests against onap (onap demos) could be handled in the pipeline (is it then ci-management) where is information what it installs (onap) by using what tools (cicdansible + offline-installer) with what datas and configurations. So providing there what tests are run and e.g. provide/configure those tests for cicdassible tool if it would support running user's given playbooks/roles. So not make cicdansibe by default run any tests.

So providing there what tests are run and e.g. provide/configure those tests for cicdassible tool if it would support running user's given playbooks/roles. So not make cicdansibe by default run any tests.

Yes, you are right. Looking at ci-management repo, it seems that it holds only jenkins jobs definitions and nothing else. Every other code at job run is retrieved from specific repo (onap component). That means the best repository for my changes should be "demo". I have already contacted integrations team if adding my code is possible and make sense. I am closing this. Thank you!
